sanskrit:
आवृतं ज्ञानम एतेन ज्ञानिनो नित्यवैरिणा।
कामरूपेण कौन्तेय दुष्पूरेणानलेन च॥
transliteration: āvṛtaṃ jñānam etena jñānino nitya-vairiṇā | kāma-rūpeṇa kaunteya duṣpūreṇānalena ca ||
literal_meaning: Knowledge is veiled by this eternal enemy in the form of desire, O son of Kuntī, which is insatiable like fire.
contextual_meaning: The mind’s innate clarity is obscured by an unquenchable craving that masquerades as legitimate need; once aroused it becomes a self-feeding loop that eclipses discrimination and keeps the person trapped in perpetual ‘wanting.’
psychological_state: Compulsive craving state: intrusive thoughts, tunnel vision on the object of desire, diminished executive control, rationalization of unethical action.
trigger_scenario: A mid-level manager promised himself one drink to celebrate a promotion; three hours later he is still at the bar, texting an ex while ignoring his family’s calls, vaguely aware he is jeopardizing everything he values.
anchor_text: See desire as a fire that never says 'enough'; starve it, don’t feed it.
